title: Web3 Starter Pack (saved reference doc by Jay Drainjr — not Yahir-authored)
html:
Web3 Starter Pack (saved reference doc by Jay Drainjr — not Yahir-authored)

Source: 2022-03-08 Google Doc. Authorship: signed by Jay Drainjr (Twitter @jay_drainjr). This is a saved reference doc — Yahir copied/saved someone else's web3 explainer to his Drive, not original work. Preserved here as evidence of Yahir's web3 learning trajectory in early 2022.

TL;DR

  • A canonical-style Web3/crypto explainer by Jay (a known crypto educator on Twitter) — Spanish + Portuguese translations linked.
  • What it covers:
    • Web1 (read) → Web2 (read-write) → Web3 (read-write-own) framing — the canonical 3-eras explainer.
    • Blockchain basics: how it works, why it matters (trust + decentralization).
    • Nodes, smart contracts, consensus mechanisms (PoW, PoS, Proof of History / Solana).
    • Ethereum-as-decentralized-supercomputer framing.
  • The doc is 3 weeks before the March 24 AI emergence note — Yahir was tracking both web3 and AI as potentially-defining moments in spring 2022.
  • Provides vocabulary anchor for Yahir's later Corvallis Crypto Club involvement, Coinbase pitches, DAO thinking, and the broader Ahmad Jamal stream crypto content.

Pattern observed

Yahir as crypto-curious in 2022 — saved this primer, joined the Corvallis Crypto Club, pitched Coinbase, eventually pulled away from crypto by 2024 in favor of AI. The web3 thread eventually died in his trajectory, but the documentation/learning-saving instinct was real. Notable that Yahir doesn't draft his OWN web3 doc — he saves Jay's instead. Same pattern with the Proven Playbook which leans heavily on David Quaid's research — Yahir builds on top of curated authorities rather than generating from scratch.

This doc is the earliest captured "saved reference" in the corpus and indicates Yahir's intellectual-curiosity-acquisition mode in early college: collect canonical primers from people 10x smarter (his words: "70%+ of it is curated from content created by people 10x smarter than me"), then build from there.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir actually read the whole thing, or just save it for future reference?
  • Did the Corvallis Crypto Club use this as onboarding material?
  • Why didn't Yahir build his own web3 explainer? (Possibly because Jay's was already canonical; same logic that makes Rankett "not invent a new religion" but industrialize what works.)

Related

  • corvallis crypto club
  • coinbase
  • dao mergers
  • 2022 03 24 thoughts on ai emergence — same spring, the AI track
  • 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream — crypto-thinking-still-active in fall
  • saved reference doc pattern

Note: this doc is preserved in raw/ and summarized here as a learning-artifact reference. Not Yahir-authored. Densify pass should treat this as secondary content vs primary.
